aquatic_beastmaster.name: Aquatic Beastmaster
description: ## Aquatic Beastmaster
‹SOURCE Blood of the Sea/22›

Hunters beneath the sea are just as committed as their surface-dwelling counterparts to working alongside their animal companions to eradicate threats. Although many aquatic beastmasters simply want to be left alone in the areas they have claimed as their own, others use their abilities to fight against aboleth masterminds, sahuagin tribes, and other villains that haunt the oceans.

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{head icon=info note="An aquatic beastmaster emulates animals found beneath the waves. This functions as ‹ability/animal focus›, but it allows only the following choices." alter="Animal focus"}

::ab[Crab]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Swim checks and a +2 competence bonus on ‹rule/grapple› combat maneuver checks.@FN1" imp8="These bonuses increase to +6 and +4, respectively." imp15="They increase to +8 and +6."}

::ab[Dolphin]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Knowledge checks to identify the abilities and weaknesses of creatures." imp8="This bonus increases to +6." imp15="It increases to +8."}
::ab[Eel]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to Dexterity." imp8="This bonus increases to +4." imp15="It increases to +6."}

::ab[Manta Ray]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Stealth checks and a +2 bonus on saving throws against poison." imp8="These bonuses increase to +6 and +4, respectively." imp15="They increase to +8 and +6."}

::ab[Octopus]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Swim checks and a +2 competence bonus on grapple combat maneuver checks.@FN1-2" imp8="These bonuses increase to +6 and +4, respectively." imp15="They increase to +8 and +6."}
::ab[Orca]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to Strength" imp8="This bonus increases to +4." imp15="It increases to +6."}

::ab[Shark]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Survival checks to track a creature to which the hunter or her animal companion has dealt damage in the past 24 hours." imp8="This bonus increases to +6." imp15="It increases to +8."}

::ab[Snake]{next icon=boost-def passive="The creature gains a +2 bonus on attack rolls when making attacks of opportunity and a +2 dodge bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity." imp8="These bonuses increases to +4." imp15="They increase to +6."}

::ab[Turtle]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to its natural armor bonus." imp8="This bonus increases to +4." imp15="It increases to +6." special="A creature without natural armor has an effective natural armor bonus of +0."}

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=down-power ability="An aquatic beastmaster's wild empathy functions only on creatures that have a swim speed or the aquatic or water subtype. However, she can improve the attitude of any such creature with Intelligence 2 or less regardless of type, including mindless creatures." alter="Wild Empathy"}

::ab[Oceanic Defense (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="An aquatic beastmaster gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against spells with the water descriptor and the extraordinary or supernatural abilities of creatures with the aquatic or water subtype whenever her animal companion is within 60 feet." replace="Improved empathic link"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[The crab and octopus foci grant the exact same bonuses. There has been no errata addressing this. If you wish to alter this with your GM, consider the following: The crab's bonuses are the same as those of the crab focus from the ‹arc-hunter/verminous hunter›. In fact, alternate animal foci from different archetypes almost always offer the exact same bonuses when they appear in different archetypes. The only exceptions are the scorpion (the verminous hunter's scorpion gives bonuses to stealth and grapple, while the ‹arc-hunter/scarab stalker› version provides defense vs poison) and the octopus. The octopus focus from the ‹arc-hunter/courtly hunter› grants bonuses to Escape Artist. The only other similarly-shaped animal focus is the ‹arc-hunter/pelagic hunter«'s› kraken focus, which grants the ‹umr/jet› ability.]{from=1 multi=2}

chameleon_adept.name: Chameleon Adept
description: ## Chameleon Adept
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/52›
Some hunters are masters of blending into every situation, even making their companions appear humanoid for brief periods of time.
::cskill{gain=Diplomacy}

::ab[Shifting Companion (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic standard="A chameleon adept can cause her companion to assume a humanoid form of the same race as herself. This functions as ‹spell/alter self›, except it is used on an adjacent animal companion to have it assume a humanoid form. The companion always assumes the same form, selected when the hunter reaches 3rd level. The hunter can change this form when she gains a new level, but the companion loses the old form and can't resume it unless the chameleon adept chooses that form when she reaches a new level." special="The companion is no more intelligent or less savage than normal; the hunter still needs to control it as normal, and it still can't activate magic items or wield manufactured weapons." useL=hunter~10 useUnit=minute useNC=10 replace="Teamwork feats~hunter's tactics"}

::ab[Terrain Chameleon (Su)]{icon=boost choice="Choose one type of terrain from the ranger's list of ‹ability/favored terrain«s›. When in the specified type of terrain, a chameleon adept gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› her hunter level on Stealth checks." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Savage Diplomacy (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="While in humanoid form, a chameleon adept's animal companion grants its master a +2 circumstance bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks. *Savage diplomacy* functions only as long as the creature being influenced can see both the companion and the master." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Precise companion"}

::ab[Animal Shape (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic standard="A chameleon adept can assume a form superficially identical to her animal companion's animal form. She gains no bonuses or natural attacks while in this form, only forms of movement. Her size category does not change. This is a polymorph effect." useL=hunter useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="She can dismiss this ability at any point as a @HLstandard_action." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Improved Shifting Companion (Su)]{l=10 icon=magic ability="A chameleon adept's animal companion improves its ability to change shape. It can use *wild shape,* as a ‹class/druid› of half the hunter's level, to gain the effects of ‹spell/beast shape I.› Unlike a druid's wild shape, this ability does not improve to encompass larger or smaller animals or elementals." replace="Raise animal companion"}

::ab[One with the Wild (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost passive="When the companion is using *wild shape* to appear as a different type of animal, it counts as being in that *animal focus* for the purpose of *one with the wild.*" alter="One with the wild"}

colluding_scoundrel.name: Colluding Scoundrel
description: ## Colluding Scoundrel
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/11›

The colluding scoundrel is a canny and conniving skirmisher, manipulating her enemies and leveraging her allies for her own benefit. A Free Captain ordering her crew to occupy foes and thus expose the enemy to her attacks might take this archetype, and colluding scoundrels have proven to be formidable members of the Wasp Queens, as their giant wasp companions draw out opponents to fall under the Wasp Queens' stinging blades. In any case, the colluding scoundrel rarely concerns herself overmuch with her allies' well-being.

:::ab{title="Scapegoat (Ex)" icon=lower flavor="A colluding scoundrel is adept at redirecting a foe's hostility toward another creature, creating openings for her own attacks." replace="Animal focus" useL=hunter useUnit=time action="Swift Action" special="This is a mind-affecting effect. The effect ends if the colluding scoundrel falls unconscious or is slain." increment="The penalty~1~7~-4~-2"}

A colluding scoundrel can target one foe within 30 feet and designate one willing ally adjacent to that foe as a scapegoat. For the following minute, the target takes a -2 penalty on attacks against any creature other than the designated scapegoat, and the target has a 10% spell failure chance on spells that don't target the scapegoat or include the scapegoat in their area of effect.
The colluding scoundrel can designate her animal companion as a scapegoat without it counting against her daily uses of this ability.

::ab[Backstabber (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost ability="A colluding scoundrel deals 2d6 additional points of damage on attacks against a target threatened by an ally currently designated as the scapegoat." imp15="This increases to 3d6 points of damage." special="This is ‹misc/precision damage› and is not multiplied on a critical hit, but it does stack with ‹ability/sneak attack› and similar effects." replace="Second animal focus"}

::ab[Master Backstabber (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power flavor="A colluding scoundrel becomes a master at dispatching distracted targets." standard="She can make a single attack against a foe that is taking the penalties of her scapegoat ability and is adjacent to an ally currently designated as the scapegoat. If the attack hits, the target takes damage normally and must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the colluding scoundrel's level + her Wisdom modifier) or be slain." special="Whether or not the target succeeds, it cannot be targeted by this ability again (by any colluding scoundrel) for 24 hours." replace="Master hunter"}

courtly_hunter.name: Courtly Hunter
description: ## Courtly Hunter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/64›

While most hunters find themselves at ease in the wildest parts of the world, courtly hunters instead make their homes in cultured urban environments. They and their animal companions are more refined than typical hunters, using their skills to navigate the winding paths of courtly intrigue as surely as other hunters find trails in the wilderness.
::cskill{gain="Bluff~Diplomacy~Sense Motive" lose="Knowledge (geography), Knowledge (nature), Survival"}

::ab[Courtly Companion]{icon=power passive="A courtly hunter's animal companion was touched by either the fey or the nature spirits that form leshys and answer to ‹spell/commune with nature›, granting it its own intelligence. The companion's type changes to ‹type/magical beast› (‹subtype/augmented› animal), but this doesn't change any of its statistics and it counts as an animal, not as a magical beast, for the purposes of determining all effects, prerequisites, and so on. It does not learn tricks, but it does have an Intelligence score equal to that of a ‹sidekick/familiar› belonging to a ‹class/wizard› of the courtly hunter's hunter level." special="Levels in other classes that grant animal companions do not stack with levels of courtly hunter for the purpose of determining the abilities of her courtly companion." alter="Animal companion"}

::ab[Refined Focus (Su)]{head icon=power ability="A courtly hunter has access to a different set of animal aspects with her animal focus. She does not gain access to the bear, bull, frog, monkey, snake, stag, tiger, or wolf ‹ability/animal_foci>«aspects›. (She still has access to the bat, falcon, mouse, and owl aspects.) Instead, she gains access to the following aspects." alter="Animal focus"}
::ab[Beaver]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Craft checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Chameleon]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Disguise checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Eagle]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to Charisma." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}
::ab[Fox]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Bluff checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Frilled Lizard]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Intimidate checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Octopus]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Escape Artist checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Raccoon]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Sleight of Hand checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Songbird]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Perform checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Subtle Companion (Su)]{l=2 icon=magic standard="A courtly hunter can transform her animal companion into a similar Tiny animal to allow it hide easily or even blend in and act as a spy. For instance, a tiger could be transformed into a housecat, a wolf could be transformed into a Tiny dog, a dire bat could be transformed into a Tiny flying fox, and so on. This functions as a polymorph effect, and the animal companion gains a +2 size bonus to Dexterity and a -4 size penalty to Strength (use ‹spelldef/the »polymorph« table› to adjust its ability score if the animal companion is larger than Medium)." replace="Precise companion"}

::ab[Skill Bond (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A courtly hunter's animal companion gains the courtly hunter's class skills as class skills. Her animal companion can use the courtly hunter's skill ranks in a skill in place of its own, but only to a maximum of the courtly hunter's hunter level (for instance, if a character had 3 levels in courtly hunter and 5 ranks in Diplomacy, her animal companion could either share 3 ranks with skill bond or use its own ranks, whichever would result in a higher total skill bonus)." replace="Hunter tactics~the hunter's bonus teamwork feats"}

::ab[Alternate Form (Su)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="The courtly hunter's animal companion learns an additional subtle form. When the courtly hunter gains this ability, she chooses a Tiny animal. When under the effects of the *subtle companion* ability, the courtly hunter's animal companion can change between a shrunken, more innocuous version of its original shape or the shape of the chosen animal as a @HLstandard_action. When it transforms, it loses abilities dependent on its original shape and gains abilities from its new shape as ‹spell/beast shape II›." imp13="The courtly hunter can select an additional form to add to the animal companion's list of choices while it is in its polymorphed form." imp19="She can select a fourth such form." replace="Bonus tricks"}

divine_hunter.name: Divine Hunter
description: ## Divine Hunter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/95›

While most hunters heed the call of nature and fight to protect its bounty, some are inspired to serve a higher power. These divine hunters use faith to aid them in their struggles, and their faith infuses their animal companions, making these companions champions of their deities.
::prereq{other="A divine hunter's alignment must be within one step of her deity's, along either the law/chaos axis or the good/evil axis. A divine hunter can otherwise be of any alignment."}
::cskill{gain="Knowledge (religion)" lose="Knowledge (dungeoneering)"}

:::ab{title=Domain icon=magic l=3 action=Ability replace="Teamwork feats" imp6="She adds the 2nd-level domain spell to her list of spells known." imp9="She adds the 3rd-level domain spell, as above." incrementPlain=She~9~3~4 incrementDesc="adds the" incrementEnd="th-level domain spell."}

A divine hunter learns to call upon the power of her deity. The divine hunter must select one ‹ability/domain<s› from those available to her deity. She gains the granted powers of this domain, using her hunter level - 2 as her cleric level for determining when the powers are gained and what effects they have. Once she chooses this domain, it cannot be changed.

If the divine hunter selects the ‹domain/animal› domain, she does not gain a second animal companion upon reaching an effective cleric level of 4th. When the divine hunter would gain that ability, her animal companion instead gains two ability score increases (gaining +1 to two different ability scores or +2 to one ability score). If her animal companion dies or is released, when she gains a new one, it benefits from this ability score increase.
In addition, the divine hunter adds the 1st-level domain spell from her domain to her list of spells known.

::ab[Otherworldly Companion (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic passive="A hunter's companion takes on otherworldly features. If the divine hunter is good (or worships a good deity), the animal companion gains the ‹template/celestial« template›. If the hunter is evil (or worships an evil deity), the animal companion gains the ‹template/fiendish« template›. If the hunter is neutral and worships a neutral deity, she must choose either the celestial or fiendish template; once this choice is made, it cannot be changed. The companion's CR is considered to be equal to its Hit Dice for the purpose of the celestial or fiendish template." replace="Hunter tactics"}

feral_hunter.name: Feral Hunter
description: ## Feral Hunter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/95›

A feral hunter has forged a bond with nature that's so strong that she doesn't merely channel the aspects of animals' she actually becomes an animal herself. Though she lacks an animal companion, a feral hunter is in tune with the beast lurking within her flesh and spirit, and lives in a near-wild state of being. A feral hunter often resembles a lycanthrope, but her power comes from her own nature and is not influenced by moonlight or silver.
::ab[Solitary]{icon=down note="Unlike most hunters, a feral hunter does not gain an animal companion."}
:::ab{title="Feral Focus (Su)" action=Ability l=1 icon=power alter="Animal focus" replace="Hunter tactics~speak with master"}

A feral hunter gains a limited ability to change her shape into hybrid animal forms. This functions as the *animal focus* class feature, except that the hunter always applies the animal aspect to herself, and there is no limit to this ability's duration. She can end this ability as a @HL[free action].

When a feral hunter uses this ability, her body takes on cosmetic aspects of an animal, such as furry skin, longer nails, elongated teeth, and oddly colored eyes; these changes do not grant her any abilities other than what is stated in the *animal focus,* and end when she takes on a different aspect or ends the ability. This physical change is a ‹spelldef/polymorph› effect, though the effects of the *animal focus* are not.

::ab[Precise Summoned Animal (Ex)]{icon=power passive="This functions like the *precise companion* class ability, except the hunter grants all her teamwork feats to all animals she summons with ‹spell/summon nature's ally›." alter="Precise companion"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A feral hunter gains the ability to change shape. This ability functions like the ‹class/druid› wild shape ability, except the hunter can take only animal forms (not elemental or plant forms). The hunter's effective druid level is equal to her class level." replace="Bonus tricks~improved empathic link~greater empathic link~master of the wild~raise animal companion"}

::ab[Summon Pack (Sp)]{l=6 icon=boost ability="Whenever a feral hunter casts a *summon nature's ally* spell to summon one or more animals, she summons one additional animal of the same type. The summoned creature or creatures must be animals and must be of the same type as the hunter's current aspect or of a similar type (bears for bear aspect, dogs or wolves for wolf aspect, great cats for the tiger aspect, and so on). The additional creature immediately vanishes if the hunter chooses a different aspect or ends his feral focus ability. She can increase the duration of any one *summon nature's ally* spell affected by this ability to 1 minute per level. She can have only one spell with a duration increased by this ability active at a time." replace="Teamwork feats gained at 6th, 9th, 12th, 15th, and 18th levels"}

feykiller.name: Feykiller
description: ## Feykiller
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/13›

Some hunters in fey-plagued regions are dedicated to tracking down and eradicating these threats. They use their connection to the natural world to ground their senses and fight corrupted First World intrusions.

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{head icon=power ability="A feykiller emulates animals that grant her the ability to unmask fey trickery. She adds the following choices to her ‹ability/animal focus› ability instead of the bear, frog, monkey, mouse, and snake choices." special="She retains the bat, bull, falcon, owl, stag, tiger, and wolf aspects." alter="Animal focus"}
::ab[Crow]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Knowledge checks to identify the abilities and weaknesses of creatures." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Goat]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 competence bonus on saving throws against enchantment spells and spell-like abilities." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}
::ab[Mongoose]{next icon=boost-def passive="The creature gains a +2 competence bonus on grapple combat maneuver checks and on saving throws against poison." increment="These bonuses~1~7~4~2"}

::ab[Shark]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Survival checks to track a creature that has taken damage from the hunter or her animal companion in the past 24 hours." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Turtle]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to its natural armor bonus." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}

::ab[Iron Talons (Ex)]{flavor="Through training and prayer, a feykiller imbues her animal companion with an enhanced ability to fight fey." icon=boost passive="One of the animal companion's natural attacks, as selected by the feykiller, is treated as cold iron for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp7="All of her animal companion's natural attacks are treated as cold iron." replace="Wild empathy~speak with master"}

::ab[Resist Nature's Lure (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="At 4th level, a feykiller gains the ‹ability/resist nature's lure› druid class feature whenever her animal companion is within 60 feet." replace="Improved empathic link"}

::ab[Grounded (Su)]{l=17 icon=def passive="A feykiller gains a +4 insight bonus on saving throws against illusion and enchantment effects, and she is immune to illusion and enchantment effects created by fey. This ability functions only when her animal companion is within 60 feet." replace="One with the wild"}

flood_flourisher.name: Flood Flourisher
description: ## Flood Flourisher
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/20›
Well adapted to their drenched surroundings, flood flourishers coordinate deadly ambushes with their loyal animal companions by capitalizing on their mastery of the soggy terrain.
::ab[Twin Hunters (Ex)]{icon=power passive="Each time a flood flourisher gains a benefit from this archetype, she also grants the benefit to her animal companion." replace="Hunter tactics"}
::ab[Skilled Ambusher (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A flood flourisher gains either ‹feat/Athletic› or ‹feat/Stealthy› as a bonus feat." replace="The teamwork feat gained at 3rd level"}

::ab[Watery Stride (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-def ability="A flood flourisher and her animal companion gain a swim speed equal to their base land speed (maximum 30 feet). A flood flourisher or animal companion that already has a swim speed instead gains a +10 bonus on Acrobatics checks to avoid provoking attacks of opportunity from swimming out of a threatened square." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Water Striker (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power ability="A flood flourisher gains the benefits of either ‹feat/Shot on the Run› or ‹feat/Spring Attack›, even if she does not meet either of the feats' prerequisites. She gains the benefits of this feat only while using her swim speed." replace="The teamwork feat gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Submerged Stealth (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost-power ability="A flood flourisher learns how to ambush targets from beneath the muck, becoming a silent threat. While in a deep bog, the flood flourisher can use cover from the water to attempt Stealth checks with no penalties from the natural environment." order=pa passive="While in a deep bog, as long as she moves no more than 5 feet in a round, she has ‹umr/tremorsense› out to a range of 30 feet against anything that moves within the same body of water. While underwater in a deep bog, she can ‹misc/hold her breath› for a number of minutes equal to 6 times her Constitution score before she risks drowning." replace="Swift tracker"}
::ab[Aquatic Action (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power ability="A flood flourisher gains the benefits of the ‹vigtalent/aquatic action› vigilante talent." replace="The teamwork feat gained at 9th level"}

::ab[Sudden Strike (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A flood flourisher can better take advantage of the element of surprise. She can take a full round's worth of actions during the surprise round, rather than a single standard action or move action." replace="The teamwork feat gained at 12th level"}

::ab[Fast Submerged Stealth (Ex)]{l=15 icon=power ability="A flood flourisher can move up to her full swim speed with no penalty while using Stealth in a deep bog, and she can maintain her tremorsense from her submerged stealth while doing so." replace="The teamwork feat gained at 15th level"}
::ab[Fast Swimmer (Ex)]{l=18 icon=boost ability="A flood flourisher increases her swim speed by 20 feet." replace="The teamwork feat gained at 18th level"}

forester.name: Forester
description: ## Forester
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/52›

While all hunters have a bond with the natural world, a forester has a stronger tie to her environment than to the animals within it. While most foresters feel strong bonds with woodland regions, the archetype functions well in other terrains as well. In such cases, a forester might refer to herself by a different name that more accurately reflects her chosen terrain. For example, a forester who favors bogs and marshes might call herself a "swamper," while one who favors frozen regions might call herself a "glacier guardian." As foresters gain levels and take on new favored terrains, they often eschew such titles completely, viewing them as unnecessary, and merely refer to themselves as guardians of the wild or champions of nature's will - but regardless of the name, their devotion remains.

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{icon=boost ability="As a forester has no animal companion, the aspects granted by this ability always apply to the forester herself, just as if a normal hunter's companion were dead." alter="Animal focus"}

::ab[Bonus Feat (Ex)]{icon=power l2="A forester gains one bonus ‹main/combat feat›." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these feat as normal." incrementDesc="gains a" incrementOrd=She~1~6 incrementEnd=" bonus combat feat." replace="Precise companion"}

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power standard="A forester can grant the benefits of one teamwork feat to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear her. Allies retain the use of this bonus feat for 3 rounds plus 1 round for every 2 levels the forester has. Allies do not need to meet the prerequisites of these bonus feats." useInc="5~hunter~2" replace="Hunter tactics"}

::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A forester can avoid even magical and unusual attacks with great agility. If she makes a successful Reflex saving throw against an attack that normally deals half damage on a successful save, she instead takes no damage. *Evasion* can be used only if the forester is wearing light armor, medium armor, or no armor. A ‹misc/helpless› forester does not gain the benefit of *evasion.*" replace="Improved empathic link"}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{icon=power-boost l=5 ability="A forester gains the ranger's ‹ability/favored terrain› ability, gaining her first such terrain." order=ap imp9="She gains a second favored terrain. In addition, the bonuses on initiative checks and skill checks in one favored terrain (including the one just selected, if so desired) increase by 2." imp13="She gains a third terrain, and chooses one terrain in which to increase the initiative and skill check bonuses by 2." imp17="She gains a fourth such terrain, and again chooses a terrain in which to increase her initiative and skill bonuses." passive="A forester adds half her favored terrain bonus on damage rolls while in her favored terrain and fighting a creature native to that terrain." replace="Animal companion"}

::ab[Camouflage (Ex)]{icon=power l=7 ability="A forester can use the Stealth skill to hide in any of her favored terrains, even if the terrain doesn't grant ‹rule/cover› or ‹rule/concealment›." replace="Bonus tricks"}
::ab[Breath of Life (Sp)]{l=10 icon=magic ability="A forester can cast ‹spell/breath of life› once per day as a spell-like ability." replace="Raise animal companion"}

::ab[Improved Evasion (Ex)]{l=11 icon=def passive="A forester's *evasion* improves. *Improved evasion* works like *evasion,* except while the forester still takes no damage on a successful Reflex saving throw against attacks, she takes only half damage on a failed save. A helpless forester does not gain the benefit of *improved evasion.*" replace="Speak with master"}
::ab[Hide in Plain Sight (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power ability="While in any of her favored terrains, a forester can use the Stealth skill even while being observed." replace="Greater empathic link"}

packmaster.name: Packmaster
description: ## Packmaster
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/96›

Some hunters form bonds with packs of well-trained creatures. Whether such a hunter is a northern berserker running with a pack of timber wolves or a savage warrior dashing through the jungle alongside her herd of dimetrodons, the packmaster revels in the thrill of the hunt and the glory of the kill. A packmaster is more comfortable in groups than alone, and although her animal companions may be weaker than a typical hunter's, what they lack in strength they make up for in numbers.
:::ab{title="Pack Bond (Ex)" icon=power action=Ability replace="Animal companion" alter="Precise companion~woodland stride~teamwork feats"}

A packmaster can have more than one animal companion, but she must divide her effective druid level between her companions to determine the abilities of each one. For example, a 4th-level packmaster can have one 4th-level animal companion, two 2nd-level companions, one 3rd-level companion and one 1st-level companion, or four 1st-level companions.

When a packmaster gains a level, she must decide how to allocate the increase among her animal companions, including whether or not to add a new 1st-level companion. Once a hunter level is allocated to a particular companion, it cannot be redistributed while that companion is in the packmaster's service. She must release the companion or wait until the companion dies to allocate its levels to another companion, which she can then do the next time she refreshes her spell slots for the day. The *share spells* animal companion ability applies to only one animal companion at a time - the packmaster cannot use it to cast a spell that affects only a single target and have the spell affect all of her animal companions.

A packmaster's *precise companion, woodland stride,* and *teamwork feats* apply to only one of her animal companions at a time. (For example, a packmaster can apply *precise companion* to one companion, *woodland stride* to another, and a given teamwork feat to a third, but cannot apply any of those to two animal companions at once.) As a @HL[swift action], she can change which companion gains any or all of these benefits.

::ab[Pack Focus (Su)]{icon=info ability="This ability functions like ‹ability/animal focus›, with the following exceptions. A packmaster can apply her animal aspect to only one of her animal companions at a time without it counting against the number of minutes per day she can use that ability. When using *animal focus* on herself or her other animal companion, the ability counts against her minutes per day as normal." special="She can have only two animal aspects in effect at a time - one that counts against her minutes per day and one that doesn't - and they can't both target the same companion. Unless both her companions are dead, the hunter can't apply the companion's aspect to herself (and thereby gain the benefit of its unlimited duration)." replace="Animal focus"}

::ab[Teamwork Feat (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="At any level at which a packmaster would gain a bonus teamwork feat, she can instead increase the number of her animal companions that gain the benefits of her *precise companion, woodland stride,* and *teamwork feats* by 1. She can select this ability multiple times." alter="Teamwork feats"}

::ab[Second Pack Focus (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="The hunter gains an ability that functions like the *second animal focus,* but the hunter can either assign each companion one aspect each or assign both aspects to the same companion. The foci on the companions don't need to be the same, nor do they need to be the same as the one assigned to the packmaster." replace="Second animal focus"}

::ab[Master of the Pack (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power-boost passive="A packmaster and her animal companions can always move at full speed while using Survival to follow tracks without penalty." order=pa ability="Each day when a packmaster gains new spells for the day, she chooses one *animal focus* to be active on herself or one of her animal companions for the entire day (if all of her animal companions are dead, she instead chooses two animal foci to be active on herself for the entire day). This focus is in addition to her *pack focus* class ability." replace="Master hunter"}

patient_ambusher.name: Patient Ambusher
description: ## Patient Ambusher
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/53;Magic Tactics Toolbox/14›

Wandering the wilds, some hunters eschew the bond of innate teamwork with their animal companions. Instead, these hunters master the creation and deployment of intricate ranger traps, often using themselves or their animal companions to lure foes into their snares.
::cskill{gain="Disable Device"}

::ab[Trapfinding (Ex)]{icon=boost-power passive="A patient ambusher adds half her hunter level (minimum +1) on Perception checks to locate traps and on Disable Device skill checks." order=pa ability="A patient ambusher can use Disable Device to disarm magic traps." replace="Nature training~wild empathy"}

::ab[Snare Trap (Ex or Su)]{icon=power ability="A patient ambusher learns how to create a snare trap. The patient ambusher can use this and other learned traps a total number of times per day equal to half her hunter level + her Wisdom modifier. This snare trap otherwise functions the same as the ‹trap/spring>ranger» snare« trap›." replace="Hunter tactics"}

::ab[Additional Traps (Ex or Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A patient ambusher can learn a new type of trap, selected from the list of ‹ability/ranger traps›. Once a trap is learned, it can't be unlearned and replaced with a different type of trap." incrementOrd=She~3~3 incrementDesc="gains a~learns a" incrementEnd=" ranger trap." special="The patient ambusher cannot learn an individual trap more than once." replace="Teamwork feats"}

::ab[Launch Trap (Su)]{l=10 icon=power order=an ability="A patient ambusher can affix a magical ranger trap to an arrow, a crossbow bolt, or a thrown weapon, allowing her to set the trap remotely or use it as a direct attack. Attaching the trap to the projectile is part of the @HLfull-round_action to create a new trap. The trapped projectile is fired or thrown in the normal manner." note="If fired at a square, the trap is treated as if the hunter had set the trap in that square, except the DC is 5 lower than normal. If the trap is fired at a creature, the target takes damage from the ranged weapon and is treated as if it had triggered the trap (attempting a saving throw, if any)." special="The attack has a maximum range of 60 feet, and penalties from range increments apply on the attack roll. The duration of the trap starts when it is created, not when it is triggered." replace="Raise animal companion"}

pelagic_hunter.name: Pelagic Hunter
description: ## Pelagic Hunter
‹SOURCE Aquatic Adventures/54›
Pelagic hunters form strong bonds that extend deep beneath the sea, reaching out to whatever heeds their call, whether it be mundane animals or something far more sinister.
::ab[Pelagic Companion]{icon=down choice="A pelagic hunter must select an aquatic animal companion." alter="Animal companion"}

::ab[Pelagic Focus (Su)]{head icon=power ability="Unlike with a normal hunter's ‹ability/animal focus›, pelagic focus allows the hunter to contact magical deep sea creatures instead of animals, using the options below instead of the normal hunter options." alter="Animal focus"}
::ab[Aboleth]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +1 morale bonus on saves against divine spells and spell-like abilities." increment="This bonus~1~7"}
::ab[Cetus]{next icon=power passive="The creature gains ‹feat/Lunge› as a bonus feat." imp12="Instead of gaining Lunge, the creature increases its reach by 5 feet."}

::ab[Charybdis]{next icon=boost passive="The creature's attacks ignore the first 5 points of hardness." imp8="This improves to ignore the first 7 points of hardness." imp15="They now ignore 10 points of hardness."}
::ab[Deep One]{next icon=def passive="The creature reduces ‹rule/pressure damage› it takes by 10." imp12="The creature is immune to damage from changing pressure."}
::ab[Hippocampus]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Swim checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Kraken]{next icon=power passive="The creature gains the ‹umr/jet› ability with a speed equal to its unmodified base land speed or four times its swim speed (if it has one), whichever is higher." imp8="If it has a swim speed, the jet ability's speed increases to five times its swim speed." imp15="If it has a swim speed, the jet's speed increases to seven times its swim speed."}
::ab[Reefclaw]{next icon=power passive="The creature gains ‹feat/Diehard› as a bonus feat." imp12="Instead of gaining Diehard, the creature gains the ‹umr/ferocity« ability›."}

::ab[Scylla]{next icon=boost passive="The creature's attacks deal 1 point of ‹rule/bleed› damage in addition to their normal damage." imp8="The bleed damage increases to 1d3." imp15="It increases to 1d6."}
::ab[Sea Serpent]{next icon=def passive="The creature is considered to be under a ‹spell/nondetection› spell it cast on itself, with a caster level equal to the pelagic hunter's class level."}
::ab[Selkie]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Bluff checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Shoggoth]{next icon=boost-def passive="The creature gains a +2 competence bonus on Perception checks and reduces the ‹rule/flanking› bonuses of creatures flanking it by 1." increment="This perception bonus~1~7~3" imp12="The creature can't be flanked."}
::ab[Siyokoy]{next icon=boost passive="The creature's attacks deal 1 additional point of electricity damage." imp8="The electricity damage increases to 1d3." imp15="It increases to 1d6."}

::ab[Blood to Water (Su)]{l=5 icon=def passive="When a pelagic hunter or her animal companion bleeds underwater into the surrounding water (whether from piercing or slashing damage, a bleed effect, or another effect that causes blood to spill), the blood transmutes into ordinary water within 1 round." special="This prevents sharks and other predators with ‹umr/keen scent› from being drawn to the pelagic hunter's position by the blood and makes it harder for such creatures to track her." replace="Woodland stride"}

plant_master.name: Plant Master
description: ## Plant Master
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/53›

Some hunters form a bond with plant life instead of an animal and take on those aspects instead. These hunters form potent bonds with plant creatures, and their leafy or fungal friends are more than capable of anything another hunter's animal allies can accomplish.

::ab[Plant Companion (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A plant master forms a mystic bond with a plant companion. A plant master can begin play with any ‹ability/druid>plant» companion<s›. Except for the companion being a creature of the plant type, this ability otherwise works like the druid's animal companion." replace="Animal companion"}

::ab[Plant Focus (Su)]{head icon=power order=uwa swift="A plant master can take on the aspect of a plant. He must select one type of plant to emulate, gaining a bonus or special ability based on the type of plant emulated and his hunter level. He can emulate only one plant at a time." useL=hunter useUnit=minute useNC=1 ability="The plant master can also apply one of these aspects to his plant companion. An aspect applied in this way does not count against the hunter's minutes of duration per day - it remains in effect until he changes it. The companion's aspect can be the same aspect the hunter has taken on or a different one." special="A plant master can select or change the plant foci on both himself and his plant companion as part of the same swift action." replace="Animal focus"}
::ab[Assassin Vine]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 bonus on combat maneuver checks to grapple." increment="This bonus~1~7~3"}

::ab[Brambles]{next icon=def passive="When the creature is hit by an unarmed strike or natural attack, the attacker takes 1 point of piercing damage." imp8="This increases to 2 points." imp15="This becomes 3 points."}
::ab[Creeping Vine]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Climb checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Giant Flytrap]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Stealth checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Mushroom]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +4 enhancement bonus on saves against poison." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Oak]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to CMD." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}

::ab[Shrieker]{next icon=power passive="The creature gains darkvision with a range of 60 feet." imp8="The range increases by 30 feet." imp15="The creature also gains ‹umr/blindsense› with a range of 10 feet."}
::ab[Spore]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Fly checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Water Lily]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Swim checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Green Empathy (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A plant master can improve the attitude of a plant creature. Green empathy functions like a Diplomacy check to improve the attitude of a person. The plant master rolls 1d20 and adds his hunter level and his Charisma modifier to determine the wild empathy check result. The typical wild plant creature has a starting attitude of indifferent. To use green empathy, the plant master and the plant creature must be within 30 feet of each other under normal conditions. Generally, influencing a plant creature in this way takes 1 minute, but as with influencing people, it might take more or less time." special="A plant master can also use this ability to influence an animal, but he takes a -4 penalty on the check. He has no ability to influence magical beasts." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Plant Shield (Ex)]{l=17 icon=def passive="A plant master and his companion are distasteful to plant creatures. Creatures of the plant type with an Intelligence score of 2 or lower will not willingly attack either the plant master or his companion unless magically compelled to do so or the plant master or his companion attacks the creature first." replace="One with the wild"}
::ab[Master Hunter (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="When a plant master prepares spells for the day, he chooses one *plant focus* to be active for the entire day." alter="Master hunter"}

primal_companion_hunter.name: Primal Companion Hunter
description: ## Primal Companion Hunter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/97›

Most hunters are skilled at awakening the primal beasts inside themselves. However, some can instead activate the primal essence within their animal companion. These primal companion hunters bestow upon their companions the ability to suddenly manifest new and terrifying powers - throwbacks to long-extinct beasts, bizarre mutations from extreme environments, or new abilities crafted from generations of selective breeding.

:::ab{l=1 action=Ability icon=power title="Primal Transformation (Su)" replace="Animal focus" useL=hunter useUnit=minute useNC=1 imp8="The number of evolution points in her pool increases to 4" imp15="It increases to 6."}

A primal companion hunter can awaken a primal creature from within his animal companion. The animal companion gains a pool of 2 evolution points that can be used to temporarily give the companion ‹ability/evolutions› as if it were an eidolon. A primal companion hunter uses her hunter level to determine her effective ‹class/summoner› level for the purpose of qualifying for evolutions and determining their effects.

Activating these evolutions on the animal companion is a @HL[swift action]. An animal companion transformed in this way cannot exceed the maximum number of attacks available to the eidolon of a summoner whose class level equals that of the hunter. While transformed in this way, the animal companion's type changes to ‹type/magical beast›, though the primal companion hunter still treats it as an animal for the purpose of the Handle Animal skill.

If a primal companion hunter's animal companion is dead, she can apply these evolutions to herself instead of to her animal companion. Uses of this ability count toward the hunter's maximum daily duration of evolution use.

::ab[Primal Surge (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost usage="Once per day" swift="A primal companion hunter can touch her animal companion and grant it one evolution that costs up to 4 evolution points. The companion must meet the prerequisites of the selected evolution. Unlike the evolutions from *primal transformation,* this evolution is not set; it can be changed each time the hunter uses this ability. Using *primal surge* activates the *primal transformation* ability on the companion if it isn't already active. This effect lasts until the hunter ends the *primal transformation.* This does not allow a companion to exceed its maximum number of natural attacks." special="This ability can grant only one evolution at a time, even if the chosen evolution could be selected multiple times. This ability can grant an evolution that allows additional evolution points to be spent to upgrade that evolution (such as ‹evolution/damage reduction› or ‹evolution/flight›), and any points left over can be spent on such upgrades. This ability cannot be used to grant an upgrade to an evolution that the companion already possesses." replace="Second animal focus"}

::ab[Primal Master (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="A primal companion hunter becomes in tune with his primal nature. He can activate his companion's primal aspect as a @HLfree_action. When using *primal surge,* he can grant his companion two evolutions instead of one (each costing up to 4 evolution points)." replace="Master hunter"}

roof_runner.name: Roof Runner
description: ## Roof Runner
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/65›

Roof runners are skilled ambushers, skirmishers, and spies who eschew heavier types of armor in order to remain light on their feet. They lurk in the shadows of the rooftops to find people or houses to rob, though some use their specialized skills for nobler purposes.

::ab[Skilled (Ex)]{icon=skill-down passive="A roof runner adds ‹skill/Acrobatics›, ‹skill/Escape Artist›, and ‹skill/Sleight of Hand› (all DEX-based) to her list of class skills." alter="Class skills" replace="Proficiency with medium armor and shields"}
::ab[Natural Leaper (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A roof runner adds half her hunter level on Acrobatics checks to jump." replace=Track}

::ab[Shingle Stride (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A roof runner and her animal companion can move at full speed without penalty when using Acrobatics to cross narrow or uneven surfaces, and climb at half speed (instead of one-quarter speed) without taking the - 5 penalty." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Alley Ghost (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A roof runner gains the benefits of the ‹talent/fast stealth› rogue talent." replace="Swift tracker"}
::ab[Master Climber (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="A roof runner gains a climb speed equal to her base land speed, instead of being able to move at full speed while tracking." alter="Master hunter"}

scarab_stalker.name: Scarab Stalker
description: ## Scarab Stalker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/55;Advanced Class Origins/13›

The forgotten ruins in the desert sands have given rise to a breed of hunters who call upon the powers of the pyramids to protect and explore the ancient sites of their people. These hunters form sacred bonds with desert-dwelling denizens.

::ab[Sacred Animal Focus (Su)]{head icon=down-power ability="A scarab stalker can select from the bull, falcon, and snake ‹ability/animal foci›, as well as the following new animal foci." alter="Animal focus"}
::ab[Bee]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Fly checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Cat]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Acrobatics checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Cow]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +1 resistance bonus on Fortitude saving throws." increment="This bonus~1~7"}
::ab[Crocodile]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Swim checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Ibis]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 resistance bonus on saving throws against disease." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}
::ab[Jackal]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Survival checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Kite]{next icon=aid passive="The creature recovers 1 additional hit point whenever it receives magical healing." imp8="This increases to 2 additional hit points." imp15="This increases to 3 additional hit points."}
::ab[Leopard]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Escape Artist checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Lion]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Intimidate checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Ram]{next icon=power passive="The creature gains a +1 bonus on damage rolls when making charge attacks." increment="This bonus~1~7"}
::ab[Scarab Beetle]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +1 enhancement bonus to its natural armor bonus." increment="This bonus~1~7"}
::ab[Scorpion]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 resistance bonus on saving throws against poison." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}
::ab[Sha]{next icon=power passive="The creature ignores the Perception penalty from sandstorms and can see twice as far in sandstorms." incrementPlain=This~1~7~3 incrementEnd="&times& as far."}
::ab[Uraeus]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 resistance bonus on saving throws against ‹umr/breath weapon«s›." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}

::ab[Desert Walker (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A scarab stalker and her animal companion can move through desert terrain at normal speed without taking damage or suffering any other impairments unless that terrain has been enchanted or magically manipulated." replace="Woodland stride"}

totem_bonded.name: Totem-Bonded
description: ## Totem-Bonded
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/131›

The ‹rule/Mammoth Lords› coexist with the great primeval beasts that roam their lands, surviving with the help of the animals' meat, hides, milk, bones, and more. These fearsome and wondrous animals have made their way into Kellid culture and tradition. Tribes are named after these creatures. Armor, clothing, weapons, and jewelry - when not fashioned from bone, hide, or teeth harvested from these beasts - are worked into images that evoke their shape and strength. They are even captured and trained for use as guardians, as hunters, or in the most iconic use, as mounts - though they're rarely tamed, as the Mammoth Lords value the ferociousness of these creatures. The sight of a Mammoth Lord atop an elephantine steed is one that few soon forget.

For some of the people of the Mammoth Lords, this adoration and respect for the megafauna that share their realm goes one step further. Shamans, oracles, druids, and others worship these animals as primal spirits of the world and draw great power from their faith. Others, like rangers and barbarians, are no less faithful in their belief, even though they focus more on physical combat than on spellcasting. Among the most devoted of these hunters are the so-called totem-bonded-hunters who are able to form a true spiritual bond with the powerful fauna that dominate the wildlands. Such a hunter embraces one of the realm's megafauna as a sort of spiritual guide, eventually attracting a mighty beast that travels with her and helps keep her territory free of invaders. By doing so, the totem-bonded hunter becomes something more than merely a guardian and provider for her tribe - she becomes a manifestation of the tribe's spiritual belief in the power of the land itself.

::ab[Primeval Companion (Ex)]{icon=power ability="The totem-bonded adopts a powerful animal native to the region - often one suited to the cold tundra - selected from the following list: ‹companion/aurochs›, ‹companion/badger›, ‹companion/bear›, ‹companion/big cat›, ‹companion/elk›, ‹companion/giant raven›, ‹companion/mastodon›, ‹companion/rhinoceros›, ‹companion/walrus›, ‹companion/wolf›, or any creature categorized as ‹family/megafauna›. At the GM's discretion, she can select other animal companions." imp7="If the animal companion's natural size is Large but it is normally available as a Medium animal companion at 7th level (such as a bear), the totem-bonded can have the companion increase in size. To generate statistics for such a creature, apply the following modifications: **Size** Large; **AC** +1 natural armor; **Ability Scores** *Str* +4, *Dex* -2, *Con* +2. Increase the damage of each of the companion's natural attacks ‹misc/by one die« size›." alter="Animal companion"}

:::ab{l=1 icon=boost action=Ability title="Shared Strength (Su)" replace="Animal focus" imp8="The totem-bonded manifests two aspects of her animal companion. The animal companion gains a +1 bonus on saving throws and a +2 bonus on checks with each skill in which it has at least 1 rank." imp15="The damage dealt by any natural attacks granted by this ability increases by two die steps. The animal companion's bonuses on saving throws and skill checks increase to +2 and +4 respectively." useL=hunter useUnit=minute useNC=1}

The connection the totem-bonded has with her animal companion allows them to draw upon one another's strength. The totem-bonded can activate this ability as a @HL[swift action]. The effect ends immediately if the animal companion is ever more than 100 feet away from the totem-bonded.
While the *shared strength* ability is active, the totem-bonded manifests one aspect of her animal companion drawn from the following list.
- Low-light vision
- ‹umr/Scent›
- +1 bonus to natural armor
- +10-foot bonus to speed
- climb speed 30 feet
- swim speed 40 feet
- 2 claws (1d4)
- bite (1d6)
- gore (1d6)
- slam (1d6)
- *The ‹umr/natural attacks› above are all primary attacks, and the listed damage is for a Medium creature.*
At 8th-level, the following aspects become available.
- +20-foot bonus to speed
- fly speed 50 feet (average)
- increased size (as ‹spell/enlarge person›)
- ‹umr/powerful charge› (doubles the damage dice dealt by the granted gore or slam attack)

The totem-bonded can select only a movement speed or natural attack that her animal companion also has, and an aspect cannot cause her speed or size to exceed that of her animal companion with this ability.

A totem-bonded's animal companion also benefits from the *shared strength* ability, and while it is active, the animal understands the totem-bonded's known languages and can be pushed to perform an unfamiliar trick as a @HL[free action].

treestrider.name: Treestrider
description: ## Treestrider
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/55›
Some hunters are masters of the wild. Whether raised by apes or having some other link with simian creatures, these hunters glide through the wilderness with ease.
::ab[Animal Companion]{icon=info note="A treestrider must select an ‹companion/ape› as her animal companion." alter="Animal companion"}

::ab[Brachiation (Ex)]{icon=power useL=hunter useUnit=minute useNC=1 free="A treestrider can gain a climb speed equal to half her base speed. In addition, she can use her climb speed to move through the canopies of forests or jungles, provided the limbs of the trees are strong enough to support her weight. The treestrider's companion gains a +10-foot enhancement bonus to its climb speed." imp8="The treestrider's climb speed increases to equal her base speed, the duration of her *brachiation* increases to 10 minutes per hunter level per day (usable in 10-minute increments), and the enhancement bonus to her companion's climb speed increases to +20 feet." imp15="She can use *brachiation* with no limit on the duration." replace="Animal focus~second animal focus"}
::ab[Improved Unarmed Strike (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A treestrider gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat." replace="Precise companion"}

::ab[Tree Stride (Sp)]{l=10 icon=magic fullround="A treestrider can move between two trees, as per the spell ‹spell/tree stride›. Unlike the spell, this ability has a duration of instantaneous and the treestrider can make only a single transfer. She can bring her animal companion with her, but if she does, her companion can't take any actions that round." useMod=Wis3 replace="Raise animal companion"}

uprooter_scout.name: Uprooter Scout
description: ## Uprooter Scout
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/13›

The Uprooters are an elite band of Kyonin elves skilled and brave enough to foray into the Tanglebriar and take the offensive in the fight against ‹monster/Treerazer›. Uprooter scouts rely on their close bonds with their animal companions to survive such raids. One Uprooter scout who accompanied an expedition into the Worldwound to put his ‹family/demon›-fighting skills to work there has retired to Mendev to teach his techniques to others, so these skills are slowly being passed on to non-elves.

::ab[Lore of the Blight (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost passive="An Uprooter scout and her animal companion gain a +2 bonus on initiative checks and on Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks when she's in ‹rule/the Abyss› or Abyss-tainted terrain (such as Tanglebriar or the Worldwound). When she travels through this terrain, she leaves no trail and can't be tracked (though she can leave a trail if she so chooses)." increment="This bonus~6~5~4~2" replace="Improved empathic link~speak with master~greater empathic link"}

::ab[Unnatural Stride (Ex)]{l=10 icon=power ability="The Uprooter scout and her animal companion learn how to move through undergrowth (as ‹ability/woodland stride›) that has been enchanted, corrupted, or magically manipulated." replace="Raise animal companion"}

::ab[Demon's End (Ex)]{l=17 icon=lower flavor="Even demons must respect the number of their kindred an Uprooter scout has slain, and lesser demons quake before her." swift="The Uprooter scout and her companion can attempt to demoralize a demon, rolling 1d20 and adding the scout's level and her Charisma modifier to determine the ‹skill/Intimidate› check result." replace="One with the wild"}

urban_hunter.name: Urban Hunter
description: ## Urban Hunter
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/25›

Urban hunters help guards track down and apprehend criminals or spies, find lost children and disaster victims, and protect animals from abuse in the city. They still draw their inspiration and strength from the natural world, marveling at how nature flourishes wherever the city allows.
::cskill{gain="Diplomacy~Knowledge (local)~Sense Motive" lose="Knowledge (nature), Survival"}
:::ab{icon=power title="Animal Companion (Ex)" action=Ability alter="Animal companion"}

Urban hunters have animal companions that are domesticated animals or animals native to a major city. The urban hunter must select her animal companion from the following list: ‹companion/axe beak›, ‹companion/badger›, ‹companion/bird›, ‹companion/camel›, ‹companion/dire rat›, ‹companion/dog›, ‹companion/horse›, ‹companion/llama›, ‹companion/ostrich›, ‹companion/pony›, ‹companion/ram›, or ‹companion/trumpeter swan›. In urban settings, an urban hunter's animal companion seems domesticated and likely harmless to anyone who has not seen it in combat (potentially reducing the DC of Diplomacy checks to convince anyone to allow it into secure areas). It draws no more attention than a friendly dog, loyal horse, or similar tame animal.

Creatures who have never seen the animal companion make an attack must succeed at a Sense Motive check (opposed by the animal companion's Stealth check) to realize the animal companion is a potential threat. The hunter can calm those who witness her animal companion's attacks (so long as they were not injured) with a successful Handle Animal check (DC = 15 + the target's Will save modifier).

::ab[Captor (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="An urban hunter and her animal companion take no penalties when dealing nonlethal damage with weapons or natural attacks." imp6="The urban hunter selects a bonus feat from the following list to be gained by both the hunter and her animal companion: ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush›, ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick›, ‹feat/Improved Disarm›, ‹feat/Improved Grapple›, and ‹feat/Improved Trip›." imp9="She selects another bonus feat from the list above." imp12="The hunter gains another bonus feat, and she can select from the list above, or any of the following feats: ‹feat/Combat Patrol›, ‹feat/Combat Reflexes›, ‹feat/Disarming Strike›, ‹feat/Greater Bull Rush›, ‹feat/Greater Dirty Trick›, ‹feat/Greater Disarm›, ‹feat/Greater Grapple›, ‹feat/Greater Trip›, and ‹feat/Lunge›." imp15="She can select another bonus feat from either list above." imp18="The hunter can gain another bonus feat from either list." special="Neither the hunter nor her animal companion needs to meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Hunter tactics~teamwork feat"}

::ab[Animal Insight (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-def flavor="An urban hunter and her animal companion learn to combine their insights to defeat deception and manipulation." passive="As long as they are within 30 feet of each other, both hunter and animal companion gain an insight bonus on Sense Motive checks equal to half the urban hunter's hunter level. They also gain a +2 insight bonus on Will saving throws against illusions and mind-affecting effects." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Frightful Ferocity (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost swift="An urban hunter and her animal companion can each attempt an Intimidate check to demoralize a foe as a swift action." replace="One with the wild"}

verminous_hunter.name: Verminous Hunter
description: ## Verminous Hunter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/98›

A verminous hunter calls on the ceaseless, single-minded dedication of vermin to hunt and overwhelm her prey. Where other hunters invoke the cunning, animalistic powers of the alpha predators, she calls on the powers of the lowest life forms, reaching out to the spider instead of the monkey, the mantis instead of the snake, or the moth instead of the owl.

::ab[Vermin Companion (Ex)]{l=1 icon=info note="A verminous hunter must choose a vermin companion instead of an animal companion. The *hunter tactics* class ability allows a verminous hunter to grant her teamwork feats to a mindless vermin companion." alter="Animal companion~hunter tactics"}
::ab[Vermin Empathy (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A verminous hunter gains the *wild empathy* ability, but can use it only to influence vermin (not animals or magical beasts)." alter="Wild empathy"}
::ab[Vermin Focus (Su)]{head icon=power ability="A verminous hunter can take on the aspect of a vermin. This functions as ‹ability/animal focus›, but allows only the following choices."}
::ab[Ant]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to Strength." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}

::ab[Beetle]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to its natural armor bonus." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2" special="A creature without natural armor has an effective natural armor bonus of +0."}

::ab[Centipede]{next icon=boost-def passive="The creature gains a +2 competence bonus on Climb checks and a +2 bonus to CMD against pull, push, reposition, and trip combat maneuvers." increment="These bonuses~1~7~4~2"}

::ab[Cockroach]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a +4 bonus on saves against cold dangers, disease, heat dangers, starvation, thirst, and on Constitution checks to avoid ‹rule/suffocation›." increment="These bonuses~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Crab]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Swim checks and a +2 competence bonus on ‹rule/grapple› combat maneuver checks." imp8="These bonuses increase to +6 and +4 respectively." imp15="They increase to +8 and +6 respectively."}
::ab[Flea]{next icon=def-boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on saves against disease and Acrobatics checks to jump." increment="These bonuses~1~7~6~2"}
::ab[Fly]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 bonus on Perception checks and on Acrobatics checks to balance or fall safely." increment="These bonuses~1~7~4~2"}

::ab[Leech]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 competence bonus on grapple combat maneuver checks, and deals 1 point of ‹rule/bleed› damage every time it succeeds at a grapple combat maneuver check to damage an opponent." imp8="These benefits increase to a +4 bonus and 3 points of bleed." imp15="The benefits increase to a +6 bonus and 5 points of bleed." special="Bleed damage from this ability does not stack with itself."}

::ab[Mantis]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 bonus on attack of opportunity attack rolls and a +2 competence bonus on grapple combat maneuver checks." increment="These bonuses~1~7~4~2"}
::ab[Moth]{next icon=power passive="The creature gains darkvision to a range of 60 feet." imp8="The range increases by 30 feet." imp15="The creature gains ‹umr/blindsense› to a range of 10 feet."}
::ab[Phasmid]{next icon=power-boost passive="The creature gains low-light vision and a +4 competence bonus on Stealth checks in forests or plains." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Scorpion]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +2 competence bonus on Stealth checks and grapple combat maneuver checks." imp8="These bonuses increase to +6 and +4 respectively." imp15="They increase to +8 and +6 respectively."}

::ab[Spider]{next icon=boost-def passive="The creature gains a +2 competence bonus on Stealth checks, saving throws against ‹spell/web› effects (magical or otherwise), and checks to break free of webs." increment="This bonus~1~7~4~2"}
::ab[Wasp]{next icon=boost passive="The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Perception checks." increment="This bonus~1~7~6~2"}

::ab[Worm]{next icon=def passive="The creature gains a 25% chance to treat any critical hit or sneak attack as a normal hit (as the ‹magic-enh/fortification› armor special ability)." imp8="This increases to 50%." imp15="This increases to 75%." replace="Animal focus"}

::ab[Swarm Stride (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def flavor="A verminous hunter learns to move through vermin without danger." ability="He can safely pass through ‹subtype/swarm«s› of vermin and does not take swarm damage while within a vermin swarm's space. In addition, he is immune to a swarm's distraction ability." special="If the hunter or his animal companion attacks a swarm, they lose this protection against only that swarm." replace="Woodland stride"}
